top_findings.finding: Participant's search for 'Pancreatic cancer and the gamma knife' returned no results, blocking access to the specific information needed.
top_findings.observed_signal: Participant attempted a search query and received no search results for the topic.
top_findings.stated_signal: still not giving me any search results for that
top_findings.rationale: The participant explicitly stated that the search yielded no results for a specific health query. Both observed (no results returned) and stated (frustration with lack of results) signals indicate the required information pathway was not accessible. This is a significant barrier to finding specific health content, matching S3 severity.

top_findings.finding: Participant could not find search results matching their query and expressed frustration that the available topic categories did not align with what they were looking for.
top_findings.observed_signal: Participant stated 'Nothing Related to what I want to search for' and 'Not able to find anything related to my search item'. They observed the available topic categories (Mental health, cancer, Muscle and bone, Parts of the body) but indicated these did not match their search intent.
top_findings.stated_signal: Nothing Related to what I want to search for. So, um Not able to find anything related to my search item information by topic.
top_findings.rationale: The participant explicitly stated they could not find information matching their search query. The observed signal shows they examined the available topic categories but found them inadequate for their need. This is a significant friction event where the required content pathway was not discoverable, fitting F6 (Content Not Found) and S3 (significant component failure or barrier), with L3 calibration reflecting the documented frustration and barrier.

top_findings.finding: Participant could not find information about brain cancer or brain tumors in the cancer section, despite understanding that tumors are a form of cancer.
top_findings.observed_signal: Participant navigated through mental health and cancer sections, recognized that brain tumors should logically fall under cancer, but found no dedicated section for brain cancer or brain tumors.
top_findings.stated_signal: There is a spot here to get information for more types of cancers because I believe tumors can come under cancer as well. A tumor is a form of cancer. However, No Sections for. Brain cancer or brain tumors.
top_findings.rationale: The participant explicitly stated that expected content (brain cancer/tumors) was not found in the location where it should logically appear. Both observed behaviour (searching relevant sections) and stated signal (expressing the absence of expected sections) confirm the content was not located. This represents a significant barrier requiring workaround or skipping, fitting S3 severity.

top_findings.finding: Participant expressed feeling overwhelmed by the volume of information on the page and stated they would resort to an AI platform instead to get specific answers.
top_findings.observed_signal: Participant verbalized hesitation about reading through the content, describing it as 'daunting,' and stated they would seek answers elsewhere via an AI platform rather than continue with the page content.
top_findings.stated_signal: I would then probably go to like an AI platform to ask specific questions to get specific answers rather than trying to read through so much information. Um, which can be a bit daunting, especially if you have a health issue.
top_findings.rationale: The participant explicitly stated they felt overwhelmed by the volume of information and expressed intent to abandon the page in favour of an external tool. This indicates excessive cognitive and navigational effort required to find specific answers on the health information page, representing a significant barrier that prompted them to consider a workaround rather than continue with the current content structure.

top_findings.finding: Participant identified the absence of a direct phone number to speak with someone as a limitation for users who find reading difficult or have unorthodox questions.
top_findings.observed_signal: Participant stated that there is no direct number to call and reflected on the implications for accessibility and non-standard inquiries.
top_findings.stated_signal: There is no chat feature on this page, but there is a search feature. There is no like direct number to call to speak to someone because reading might not be feasible for everyone. Daunting and like I said, some of the questions you might have might be a bit unorthodox.
top_findings.rationale: The participant recognized that a critical pathway—direct contact with a human agent by phone—is not available on the page. This is a content/pathway absence that would be significant for users with reading difficulties or non-standard needs. The participant expressed concern about feasibility and daunting nature, indicating frustration with the constraint, though they also acknowledged understanding the trade-off.
top_findings.structural_amplification_note: Users with reading difficulties, cognitive disabilities, or those with atypical health concerns would experience structurally higher friction due to the absence of direct phone contact as an alternative to text-based resources.

coaching_recommendations.title: Review high-friction moments before lower-level issues
coaching_recommendations.summary: The session contains 63 finding-level issue(s), with the highest severity at S3.
advice: Identify the common pattern behind the higher-severity findings.
Focus coaching on the most disruptive parts of the session first.
Avoid treating all findings as equal when severity levels differ.
coaching_recommendations.evidence_note: 5.1-A severity distribution: S3=9, S4=4, S5=43, S6=7. Representative findings: Participant's search for 'Pancreatic cancer and the gamma knife' returned no results, blocking access to the specific information needed. | Participant could not find search results matching their query and expressed frustration that the available topic categories did not align with what they were looking for. | Participant could not find information about brain cancer or brain tumors in the cancer section, despite understanding that tumors are a form of cancer.

coaching_recommendations.title: Multi-pattern friction across 7 categories — anchor coaching on the dominant pattern
coaching_recommendations.summary: This session shows 63 findings spanning 7 distinct friction types (F6,F1,F7,F2,F5,F4,F3). Coaching should follow the dominant pattern first rather than issuing one item per type.
advice: After addressing any severity-priority recommendation first, use this aggregation to group the remaining coaching themes — do not treat the two as parallel.
Treat F6 (Not Found) as the primary coaching anchor; secondary types (F1 Comprehension, F7 Excessive Effort) provide supporting context.
Avoid issuing parallel recommendations for each friction type when patterns interact within the same session.
coaching_recommendations.evidence_note: 5.1-A: total=63 findings across 7 distinct friction types (F6=22, F1=14, F7=10, F2=5, F5=5, F4=4, F3=3).
